differentials.name: months; M÷F = 1÷1; no race predilection
differentials.raw: months; M÷F = 1÷1; no race predilection

differentials.name: Sporadic postzygotic somatic mutation
differentials.raw: Sporadic postzygotic somatic mutation (subsequent generations unaffected)

differentials.name: Chromosomal anomaly =  monosomy 13 / deletions of 13q
differentials.raw: Chromosomal anomaly =  monosomy 13 / deletions of 13q

differentials.name: Heritable sporadic form
differentials.raw: Heritable sporadic form (20-25%) =  sporadic germline mutation (50% chance to occur in subsequent generations)

differentials.name: Familial retinoblastoma
differentials.raw: Familial retinoblastoma (10-30%)

differentials.name: Exophytic form = growth toward sclera into subretinal space → retinal detachment + invasion of vascular choroid → hematogenous spread
differentials.raw: Exophytic form = growth toward sclera into subretinal space → retinal detachment + invasion of vascular choroid → hematogenous spread

differentials.name: Mixture of endo- and exophytic growth
differentials.raw: Mixture of endo- and exophytic growth (common)

differentials.name: Diffuse infiltrating form = thin en-plaque lesion extending along retina
differentials.raw: Diffuse infiltrating form = thin en-plaque lesion extending along retina (in 1-2%) → cells discharged into vitreus → seeding of anterior chamber mimicking an inflammatory process (= pseudohypopyon)

differentials.name: Necrotic retinoblastoma with orbital cellulitis
differentials.raw: Necrotic retinoblastoma with orbital cellulitis

differentials.name: Complete spontaneous regression
differentials.raw: Complete spontaneous regression (rare) → phthisis bulbi (= shrunken nonfunctioning globe)

differentials.name: "cat's eye" = leukokoria (white pupillary reflex) in 56-72% ◊  About 50% of all childhood leukokoria cases are caused by retinoblastoma!
differentials.raw: "cat's eye" = leukokoria (white pupillary reflex) in 56-72% ◊  About 50% of all childhood leukokoria cases are caused by retinoblastoma!

differentials.name: strabismus = crossed eyes = lack of binocular vision
differentials.raw: strabismus = crossed eyes = lack of binocular vision (22-24%)

differentials.name: proptosis
differentials.raw: proptosis (less common)

differentials.name: decreased visual acuity, heterochromia iridis, anisokoria
differentials.raw: decreased visual acuity, heterochromia iridis, anisokoria

differentials.name: spontaneous hyphema, periocular inflammation
differentials.raw: spontaneous hyphema, periocular inflammation

differentials.name: iris neovascularization
differentials.raw: iris neovascularization

differentials.name: ocular pain ← secondary angle-closure glaucoma Location:   posterolateral wall of globe
differentials.raw: ocular pain ← secondary angle-closure glaucoma Location:   posterolateral wall of globe (most commonly); 60% unilateral; 40% bilateral + frequently synchronous (90% bilateral in inherited forms)

differentials.name: Radiation-induced sarcomas develop in 15-20%
differentials.raw: Radiation-induced sarcomas develop in 15-20%

differentials.name: Persistent fetal vasculature (no calcifications, microphthalmia, vertical septum
differentials.raw: Persistent fetal vasculature (no calcifications, microphthalmia, vertical septum

differentials.name: Coats disease
differentials.raw: Coats disease (subretinal exudation, no calcification, slightly older age group, no enhancement of subretinal space)

differentials.name: Toxocara endophthalmitis
differentials.raw: Toxocara endophthalmitis (no calcification, usually > 5 years of age, contact with dogs, serology)

differentials.name: Retrolental fibroplasia
differentials.raw: Retrolental fibroplasia (microphthalmia, bilateral, low birth weight, periventricular leukomalacia)

differentials.name: Giant drusen = nodular type of retinal astrocytic hamartoma
differentials.raw: Giant drusen = nodular type of retinal astrocytic hamartoma (confined to sensory retina / optic disc, often lack hemorrhage / necrosis, may be associated with tuberous sclerosis / NF1)

differentials.name: Norrie disease
differentials.raw: Norrie disease (retinal dysplasia)

differentials.name: Coloboma of choroid / optic disc coloboma
differentials.raw: Coloboma of choroid / optic disc coloboma

differentials.name: Retinoma = retinocytoma
differentials.raw: Retinoma = retinocytoma (benign variant)

context: =  NEUROEPITHELIOMA OF RETINA =  rare aggressive malignant congenital intraocular tumor Prevalence: 1÷18,000 children < 5 years old in USA; 1÷17,000 - 1÷24,000 live births ◊ Most common intraocular childhood malignancy Frequency:   1% of all pediatric malignancies; 11% of all cancers in 1st year of life; in 80% diagnosed < 3 years of age; in 95% < 5 years of age Origin:   primitive photoreceptor cells of immature retina (included in primitive neuroectodermal tumor group) Cause:   biallelic mutation (= loss of function of both alleles) in primitive neuroepithelial cells located on long arm of c
